meta.name: playwright-typescript
meta.title: Playwright Typescript Skill
meta.description: Playwright test generation instructions Triggers on: **

original_definition.skill_description: Playwright test generation instructions Triggers on: **

actions.name: Imports: Start with `import { test, expect } from '@playwrig
actions.description: Imports: Start with `import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';`.

actions.name: Organization: Group related tests for a feature under a `tes
actions.description: Organization: Group related tests for a feature under a `test.describe()` block.

actions.name: Hooks: Use `beforeEach` for setup actions common to all test
actions.description: Hooks: Use `beforeEach` for setup actions common to all tests in a `describe` block (e.g., navigating to a page).

actions.name: Location: Store all test files in the `tests/` directory.
actions.description: Location: Store all test files in the `tests/` directory.

actions.name: Scope: Aim for one test file per major application feature o
actions.description: Scope: Aim for one test file per major application feature or page.

actions.name: // Navigate to the application before each test
actions.description: // Navigate to the application before each test

actions.description: test('Search for a movie by title', async ({ page }) => {

actions.description: await test.step('Activate and perform search', async () => {

actions.name: [ ] Tests are grouped logically and follow a clear structure
actions.description: [ ] Tests are grouped logically and follow a clear structure

actions.name: [ ] Code is properly formatted and commented
actions.description: [ ] Code is properly formatted and commented

rules.description: // Verify the accessibility tree of the search results
[ ] All locators are accessible and specific and avoid strict mode violations

directives.description: Locators: Prioritize user-facing, role-based locators (`getByRole`, `getByLabel`, `getByText`, etc.) for resilience and accessibility. Use `test.step(
Assertions: Use auto-retrying web-first assertions. These assertions start with the `await` keyword (e.g., `await expect(locator).toHaveText()`). Avoi
Timeouts: Rely on Playwright's built-in auto-waiting mechanisms. Avoid hard-coded waits or increased default timeouts.
Clarity: Use descriptive test and step titles that clearly state the intent. Add comments only to explain complex logic or non-obvious interactions.
Titles: Follow a clear naming convention, such as `Feature - Specific action or scenario`.
UI Structure: Use `toMatchAriaSnapshot` to verify the accessibility tree structure of a component. This provides a comprehensive and accessible snapsh
Element Counts: Use `toHaveCount` to assert the number of elements found by a locator.
Text Content: Use `toHaveText` for exact text matches and `toContainText` for partial matches.
Navigation: Use `toHaveURL` to verify the page URL after an action.
